Employment Scams

Guides covering fake job ads, work-from-home schemes, and advance-fee employment fraud.

Employment Scams

Job Offer Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Job offer scams deceive UK applicants by posing as legitimate employers, often requesting upfront payments for training, equipment, or background checks. This guide explains common scam tactics, warning signs to watch for, and practical steps to verify job opportunities before committing time or money.

Updated 2026-04-17

Employment Scams

Work From Home Scams in the UK: How to Spot and Avoid Them

Work from home opportunities attract millions of UK job seekers, but scammers exploit this demand with fake job postings and fraudulent schemes. This guide explains the most common work from home scams, red flags to watch for, how scammers operate, and practical steps to verify legitimate opportunities before applying or paying money.

Updated 2026-04-17

Employment Scams

LinkedIn Job Scams: How to Spot and Avoid Them in the UK

A comprehensive guide to understanding LinkedIn job scams targeting UK job seekers. This article explains common tactics used by scammers, warning signs to watch for, steps to verify legitimate opportunities, and how to report fraudulent activity to LinkedIn and relevant authorities.

Updated 2026-04-17

Employment Scams

Job Scam Checklist UK: Warning Signs Before You Share Documents or Pay Fees

How to assess suspicious job offers, recruiter messages, and work-from-home opportunities in the UK.

Updated 2026-04-02

Scam alerts, plain-English checks, and practical guides to help people verify suspicious emails, texts, websites, calls, and offers.
